factions.name: The Dustmen
factions.description: Believe all life is a prelude to True Death. Control the Mortuary in Sigil — where unclaimed dead are processed. The party woke up here. Suspicious of anyone too alive.

factions.name: The Harmonium
factions.description: Sigil's de facto law enforcement. Believe universal peace is achievable through compliance. Rigid, aggressive, and not above brutality. Cross them and they will remember.

factions.name: The Fraternity of Order
factions.description: Known as the Guvners. Study and exploit the laws of the multiverse — legal and metaphysical. Useful for navigating Sigil's bureaucracy. Everything has a rule; they know it.

factions.name: The Mercykillers
factions.description: Justice through punishment, without mercy. Run Sigil's prison. If the party commits crimes and gets caught, these are the ones who come for them.

factions.name: The Bleak Cabal
factions.description: The Bleakers. Believe nothing in the multiverse has inherent meaning. Operate the asylum. Surprisingly compassionate in practice — they help because they choose to, not because it matters.

factions.name: The Society of Sensation
factions.description: Believe truth is found through experience. Collect and catalog every sensation across the planes. Hedonistic and well-connected. Can be useful allies if the party is interesting enough.

factions.name: The Sign of One
factions.description: The Signers. Believe each individual is the center of their own reality and may shape existence through will. Relevant to the multiversal glitch in ways that will become clear.

factions.name: The Athar
factions.description: Deny the divinity of gods — view them as powerful beings, not true deities. Have hidden knowledge about the nature of the planes that may be relevant to the party's situation.
